title: hepetis platypetala
intro: Hepetis platypetala, also known as the "Indian tobacco" or "poor man's tobacco", is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Hepetis platypetala has various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cough and sore throats, and aiding in digestion. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
